message:
fix(unix): hold lock across driver.register() to prevent EEXIST race in split streams

When both the read and write halves of a split UnixStream concurrently
entered ensure_registration(), each could see no existing registration
and attempt epoll_ctl(ADD) on the same fd. The second ADD would fail
with EEXIST because the lock was dropped before calling driver.register().

Fixed by keeping the MutexGuard alive across the register() call and
storing the registration directly into the guarded state. The comment
now documents the invariant: the lock prevents concurrent ADD attempts
for the same fd.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
